event: 33-year-old
Unmarried
Nullipara
Brother diagnosed with testicular cancer
At the age of 28 years
Underwent explorative laparoscopy due to nonspecific abdominal pain
Gelatinous tumor spread was detected
Biopsy specimens confirmed disseminated peritoneal adenomucinosis (DPAM)
Peritoneal lesions originated from perforated mucinous cystadenoma of the appendix
Advised to undergo peritonectomy
Advised to undergo bilateral oophorectomy
Referred for consultation on fertility preservation options
Decided to undergo IVF and embryo cryopreservation procedures
Twenty oocytes were retrieved
18 embryos were fertilized with donor sperm
18 embryos were cryopreserved at the 2 pronuclear stage
Institutional Ethical Committee approved embryo cryopreservation procedure
PMP surgery was performed by a multidisciplinary team
Extended spread of the disease was found in the Douglas pouch
Extended spread of the disease was found over the omentum
Extended spread of the disease was found underneath the diaphragm
Extended spread of the disease was found over the spleen
Two large masses on both ovaries were found
A mass on the appendix was found
A number of lesions over the surface of the uterus were found
Parietal peritoneum was resected
Omentectomy was performed
Appendectomy was performed
Splenectomy was performed
Bilateral adnexectomy was performed
Decided to leave the uterus intact to preserve future fertility
Received heated intraperitoneal infusions of mitomycin C
Eighteen month later
Disease relapsed
Moderate elevation of carcinoembrionic antigen (CEA)
Intra abdominal fluid accumulation with multiple peritoneal lesions by computed tomography
Explorative laparotomy showed numerous small lesions over the peritoneum
Explorative laparotomy showed lesions over the left diaphragm
Explorative laparotomy showed a lesion over the posterior wall the uterus
Underwent cytoreduction
Underwent peritonectomy
Received a 5-day intraperitoneal infusion of 5-fluorouracil (600 mg/m2/day)
At the age of 37 years
Expressed desire to become pregnant
Endometrium preparation based on administration of 2 mg tid E2 valerate
Vaginal administration of micronized progesterone (200 mg tid) was added when endometrial thickness reached 8 mm
Three of the 9 surviving thawed embryos were transferred
hCG was positive 12 days later
hCG doubled within 48 hours
At 21 weeks' gestation, diagnosed with deep vein thrombosis (DVT)
Started on enoxaparin
Difficult to anatomically identify the uterine cervix on ultrasonography
During gestational week 28, presented with left flank pain
Gradual elevation of creatinine (up to 1.4 mL/DL)
Single course of Betametasone (2 doses of 12 mg, IM-24 hours apart) was given for fetal lung maturation
Abdominal USG demonstrated mild bilateral hydronephrosis
No evidence of urine jets
Urinary bladder covered the anterior part of the uterus
Doppler USG ruled out renal vein thrombosis
Cystoscopy revealed a much distorted bladder trigone
Pigtail (6 F/26 cm) was inserted into the left ureter
Symptoms gradually resolved
At gestational week 30, suffered from urosepsis
Under intravenous antibiotic treatment her condition was markedly improved
At gestational week 32, again complained of left flank pain
Experiencing difficulty in urinating
Blood creatinine had sharply risen to 3.1 mL/DL
Urology consultant suggested nephrostomy by angiography
It was decided to deliver the fetus due to the deterioration of the patient's condition
Received oxytocin to induce labor (bishop score, 7)
Healthy male neonate weighing 1,545 g was delivered vaginally
Neonate transferred to the neonatal intensive care unit
Reported immediate relief of the flank pain
Could urinate at will immediately after delivery
Received 2 infusions of packed cells due to postpartum hemorrhage
Blood creatinine level returned to normal
Put on warfarin
Discharged home
